contentBlocks.original: श्रीभैरव उवाच
contentBlocks.transliteration: śrībhairava uvācha
contentBlocks.translation: Śrī Bhairava said:

contentBlocks.original:
यो देवदेवो भगवान् भास्करो महसां निधिः ।
गयत्रीनायको भास्वान् सवितेति प्रगीयते ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yō dēvadēvō bhagavān bhāskarō mahasāṃ nidhiḥ ।
gayatrīnāyakō bhāsvān savitēti pragīyatē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who is the God of gods, the resplendent Lord Bhāskara, the treasure-house of luminaries, the master of the Gāyatrī, the radiant one — he is celebrated as Savitṛ. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
तस्याहं कवचं दिव्यं वज्रपञ्जरकाभिधम् ।
सर्वमन्त्रमयं गुह्यं मूलविद्यारहस्यकम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasyāhaṃ kavachaṃ divyaṃ vajrapañjarakābhidham ।
sarvamantramayaṃ guhyaṃ mūlavidyārahasyakam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: His divine armor, known as the Vajra Pañjara ("Diamond Cage"), I shall reveal — composed entirely of mantras, secret, the innermost mystery of the root-knowledge. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वपापापहं देवि दुःखदारिद्र्यनाशनम् ।
महाकुष्ठहरं पुण्यं सर्वरोगनिवर्हणम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapāpāpahaṃ dēvi duḥkhadāridryanāśanam ।
mahākuṣṭhaharaṃ puṇyaṃ sarvarōganivarhaṇam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, it removes all sins, destroys suffering and poverty, cures great leprosy, is meritorious, and wards off all diseases. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वशत्रुसमूहघ्नं सम्ग्रामे विजयप्रदम् ।
सर्वतेजोमयं सर्वदेवदानवपूजितम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvaśatrusamūhaghnaṃ samgrāmē vijayapradam ।
sarvatējōmayaṃ sarvadēvadānavapūjitam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: It slays the entire host of enemies, bestows victory in battle, is made of all-encompassing radiance, and is worshipped by all gods and demons. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
रणे राजभये घोरे सर्वोपद्रवनाशनम् ।
मातृकावेष्टितं वर्म भैरवानननिर्गतम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇē rājabhayē ghōrē sarvōpadravanāśanam ।
mātṛkāvēṣṭitaṃ varma bhairavānananirgatam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Destroyer of all calamities in battle, in the terrible fear of kings, and of all afflictions — this armor, encircled by the mother-syllables (mātṛkās), has issued from the face of Bhairava. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
ग्रहपीडाहरं देवि सर्वसङ्कटनाशनम् ।
धारणादस्य देवेशि ब्रह्मा लोकपितामहः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
grahapīḍāharaṃ dēvi sarvasaṅkaṭanāśanam ।
dhāraṇādasya dēvēśi brahmā lōkapitāmahaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, it removes afflictions from the planets and destroys all dangers. By wearing this armor, O Queen of the gods, Brahmā the grandfather of all worlds — (6)

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुर्नारायणो देवि रणे दैत्याञ्जिष्यति ।
शङ्करः सर्वलोकेशो वासवोऽपि दिवस्पतिः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇurnārāyaṇō dēvi raṇē daityāñjiṣyati ।
śaṅkaraḥ sarvalōkēśō vāsavō'pi divaspatiḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — Viṣṇu Nārāyaṇa conquered the demons in battle, O Goddess; Śaṅkara the lord of all worlds, and Vāsava the lord of heaven — (7)

contentBlocks.original:
ओषधीशः शशी देवि शिवोऽहं भैरवेश्वरः ।
मन्त्रात्मकं परं वर्म सवितुः सारमुत्तमम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṣadhīśaḥ śaśī dēvi śivō'haṃ bhairavēśvaraḥ ।
mantrātmakaṃ paraṃ varma savituḥ sāramuttamam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — the lord of herbs the Moon, and I myself, Śiva, Bhairavēśvara. This armor, whose very essence is mantra, is the supreme quintessence of Savitṛ. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
यो धारयेद् भुजे मूर्ध्नि रविवारे महेश्वरि ।
स राजवल्लभो लोके तेजस्वी वैरिमर्दनः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yō dhārayēd bhujē mūrdhni ravivārē mahēśvari ।
sa rājavallabhō lōkē tējasvī vairimardanaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever wears it on the arm or crown of the head on a Sunday, O Mahēśvarī, becomes beloved of kings in the world, radiant, and a crusher of enemies. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
बहुनोक्तेन किं देवि कवचस्यास्य धारणात् ।
इह लक्ष्मीधनारोग्य-वृद्धिर्भवति नान्यथा ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bahunōktēna kiṃ dēvi kavachasyāsya dhāraṇāt ।
iha lakṣmīdhanārōgya-vṛddhirbhavati nānyathā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Why say more, O Goddess? By wearing this armor, prosperity, wealth, health, and growth come in this life — it is no otherwise. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
परत्र परमा मुक्तिर्देवानामपि दुर्लभा ।
कवचस्यास्य देवेशि मूलविद्यामयस्य च ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paratra paramā muktirdēvānāmapi durlabhā ।
kavachasyāsya dēvēśi mūlavidyāmayasya cha ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: And in the hereafter, the supreme liberation — hard to obtain even for the gods — is attained through this armor, O Queen of gods, which is made of the root-knowledge. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
वज्रपञ्जरकाख्यस्य मुनिर्ब्रह्मा समीरितः ।
गायत्र्यं छन्द इत्युक्तं देवता सविता स्मृतः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vajrapañjarakākhyasya munirbrahmā samīritaḥ ।
gāyatryaṃ Chanda ityuktaṃ dēvatā savitā smṛtaḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): The sage of this armor called the Diamond Cage is declared to be Brahmā; the metre is said to be Gāyatrī; the presiding deity is remembered as Savitṛ. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
माया बीजं शरत् शक्तिर्नमः कीलकमीश्वरि ।
सर्वार्थसाधने देवि विनियोगः प्रकीर्तितः ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māyā bījaṃ śarat śaktirnamaḥ kīlakamīśvari ।
sarvārthasādhanē dēvi viniyōgaḥ prakīrtitaḥ ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Māyā (Hrīṃ) is the seed-syllable; Śarat (Hrīṃ) is the power; "Namaḥ" is the pin, O Īśvarī. Its application is proclaimed for the attainment of all goals. (13)

contentBlocks.original: अथ सूर्य कवचं
contentBlocks.transliteration: atha sūrya kavachaṃ
contentBlocks.translation: Now the Sūrya Kavacham (Armor of the Sun):

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ अं आं इं ईं शिरः पातु ॐ सूर्यो मन्त्रविग्रहः ।
उं ऊं ऋं ॠं ललाटं मे ह्रां रविः पातु चिन्मयः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ aṃ āṃ iṃ īṃ śiraḥ pātu ōṃ sūryō mantravigrahaḥ ।
uṃ ūṃ ṛṃ ṝṃ lalāṭaṃ mē hrāṃ raviḥ pātu chinmayaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ aṃ āṃ iṃ īṃ — may Sūrya, the embodiment of mantra, protect my head. Uṃ ūṃ ṛṃ ṝṃ — may Ravi, pure consciousness (chinmaya), protect my forehead. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
~लुं ~लूं एं ऐं पातु नेत्रे ह्रीं ममारुणसारथिः ।
ॐ औं अं अः श्रुती पातु सः सर्वजगदीश्वरः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
~ḻuṃ ~ḻūṃ ēṃ aiṃ pātu nētrē hrīṃ mamāruṇasārathiḥ ।
ōṃ auṃ aṃ aḥ śrutī pātu saḥ sarvajagadīśvaraḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ḻuṃ ḻūṃ ēṃ aiṃ — may the one whose charioteer is Aruṇa, with Hrīṃ, protect my eyes. Oṃ auṃ aṃ aḥ — may the Lord of all the worlds protect my ears. (15)

contentBlocks.original:
कं खं गं घं पातु गण्डौ सूं सूरः सुरपूजितः ।
चं छं जं झं च नासां मे पातु यारं अर्यमा प्रभुः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṃ khaṃ gaṃ ghaṃ pātu gaṇḍau sūṃ sūraḥ surapūjitaḥ ।
chaṃ Chaṃ jaṃ jhaṃ cha nāsāṃ mē pātu yārṃ aryamā prabhuḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kaṃ khaṃ gaṃ ghaṃ — may Sūra, worshipped by the gods, protect my cheeks. Chaṃ chaṃ jaṃ jhaṃ — may Lord Aryamā protect my nose. (16)

contentBlocks.original:
टं ठं डं ढं मुखं पायाद् यं योगीश्वरपूजितः ।
तं थं दं धं गलं पातु नं नारायणवल्लभः ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṭaṃ ṭhaṃ ḍaṃ ḍhaṃ mukhaṃ pāyād yaṃ yōgīśvarapūjitaḥ ।
taṃ thaṃ daṃ dhaṃ galaṃ pātu naṃ nārāyaṇavallabhaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ṭaṃ ṭhaṃ ḍaṃ ḍhaṃ — may the one worshipped by the Lord of Yogis protect my face. Taṃ thaṃ daṃ dhaṃ — may the beloved of Nārāyaṇa protect my throat. (17)

contentBlocks.original:
पं फं बं भं मम स्कन्धौ पातु मं महसां निधिः ।
यं रं लं वं भुजौ पातु मूलं सकनायकः ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paṃ phaṃ baṃ bhaṃ mama skandhau pātu maṃ mahasāṃ nidhiḥ ।
yaṃ raṃ laṃ vaṃ bhujau pātu mūlaṃ sakanāyakaḥ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Paṃ phaṃ baṃ bhaṃ — may the treasure-house of lights protect my shoulders. Yaṃ raṃ laṃ vaṃ — may the lord Sakanāyaka protect my arms. (18)

contentBlocks.original:
शं षं सं हं पातु वक्षो मूलमन्त्रमयो ध्रुवः ।
लं क्षः कुक्ष्सिं सदा पातु ग्रहाथो दिनेश्वरः ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṃ ṣaṃ saṃ haṃ pātu vakṣō mūlamantramayō dhruvaḥ ।
ḻaṃ kṣaḥ kukṣsiṃ sadā pātu grahāthō dinēśvaraḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śaṃ ṣaṃ saṃ haṃ — may the immovable one (Dhruva), the embodiment of root-mantra, protect my chest. Ḻaṃ kṣaḥ — may the lord of the planets, the day-lord, always protect my abdomen. (19)

contentBlocks.original:
ङं ञं णं नं मं मे पातु पृष्ठं दिवसनायकः ।
अं आं इं ईं उं ऊं ऋं ॠं नाभिं पातु तमोपहः ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṅaṃ ñaṃ ṇaṃ naṃ maṃ mē pātu pṛṣṭhaṃ divasanāyakaḥ ।
aṃ āṃ iṃ īṃ uṃ ūṃ ṛṃ ṝṃ nābhiṃ pātu tamōpahaḥ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ṅaṃ ñaṃ ṇaṃ naṃ maṃ — may the leader of the day protect my back. Aṃ āṃ iṃ īṃ uṃ ūṃ ṛṃ ṝṃ — may the destroyer of darkness protect my navel. (20)

contentBlocks.original:
~लुं ~लूं एं ऐं ॐ औं अं अः लिङ्गं मेऽव्याद् ग्रहेश्वरः ।
कं खं गं घं चं छं जं झं कटिं भानुर्ममावतु ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
~ḻuṃ ~ḻūṃ ēṃ aiṃ ōṃ auṃ aṃ aḥ liṅgaṃ mē'vyād grahēśvaraḥ ।
kaṃ khaṃ gaṃ ghaṃ chaṃ Chaṃ jaṃ jhaṃ kaṭiṃ bhānurmamāvatu ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ḻuṃ ḻūṃ ēṃ aiṃ oṃ auṃ aṃ aḥ — may the lord of planets guard my generative region. Kaṃ khaṃ gaṃ ghaṃ chaṃ chaṃ jaṃ jhaṃ — may Bhānu protect my waist. (21)

contentBlocks.original:
टं ठं डं ढं तं थं दं धं जानू भास्वान् ममावतु ।
पं फं बं भं यं रं लं वं जङ्घे मेऽव्याद् विभाकरः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṭaṃ ṭhaṃ ḍaṃ ḍhaṃ taṃ thaṃ daṃ dhaṃ jānū bhāsvān mamāvatu ।
paṃ phaṃ baṃ bhaṃ yaṃ raṃ laṃ vaṃ jaṅghē mē'vyād vibhākaraḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ṭaṃ ṭhaṃ ḍaṃ ḍhaṃ taṃ thaṃ daṃ dhaṃ — may Bhāsvān protect my knees. Paṃ phaṃ baṃ bhaṃ yaṃ raṃ laṃ vaṃ — may Vibhākara protect my calves. (22)

contentBlocks.original:
शं षं सं हं लं क्षः पातु मूलं पादौ त्रयितनुः ।
ङं ञं णं नं मं मे पातु सविता सकलं वपुः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṃ ṣaṃ saṃ haṃ ḻaṃ kṣaḥ pātu mūlaṃ pādau trayitanuḥ ।
ṅaṃ ñaṃ ṇaṃ naṃ maṃ mē pātu savitā sakalaṃ vapuḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śaṃ ṣaṃ saṃ haṃ ḻaṃ kṣaḥ — may Trayītanu (whose body is the threefold Veda) protect my feet. Ṅaṃ ñaṃ ṇaṃ naṃ maṃ — may Savitṛ protect my entire body. (23)

contentBlocks.original:
सोमः पूर्वे च मां पातु भौमोऽग्नौ मां सदावतु ।
बुधो मां दक्षिणे पातु नैऋत्या गुररेव माम् ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sōmaḥ pūrvē cha māṃ pātu bhaumō'gnau māṃ sadāvatu ।
budhō māṃ dakṣiṇē pātu naiṛtyā gurarēva mām ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Soma (the Moon) protect me in the East; may Bhauma (Mars) always protect me in the Southeast. May Budha (Mercury) protect me in the South; may Guru (Jupiter) protect me in the Southwest. (24)

contentBlocks.original:
पश्चिमे मां सितः पातु वायव्यां मां शनैश्चरः ।
उत्तरे मां तमः पायादैशान्यां मां शिखी तथा ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paśchimē māṃ sitaḥ pātu vāyavyāṃ māṃ śanaiścharaḥ ।
uttarē māṃ tamaḥ pāyādaiśānyāṃ māṃ śikhī tathā ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Sita (Venus) protect me in the West; may Śanaiścara (Saturn) protect me in the Northwest. May Tamas (Rāhu) protect me in the North; and may Śikhī (Ketu) protect me in the Northeast. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
ऊर्ध्वं मां पातु मिहिरो मामधस्ताञ्जगत्पतिः ।
प्रभाते भास्करः पातु मध्याह्ने मां दिनेश्वरः ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ūrdhvaṃ māṃ pātu mihirō māmadhastāñjagatpatiḥ ।
prabhātē bhāskaraḥ pātu madhyāhnē māṃ dinēśvaraḥ ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May Mihira (the Sun) protect me above; may the Lord of the world protect me below. May Bhāskara protect me at dawn; may Dinēśvara protect me at midday. (26)

contentBlocks.original:
सायं वेदप्रियः पातु निशीथे विस्फुरापतिः ।
सर्वत्र सर्वदा सूर्यः पातु मां चक्रनायकः ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāyaṃ vēdapriyaḥ pātu niśīthē visphurāpatiḥ ।
sarvatra sarvadā sūryaḥ pātu māṃ chakranāyakaḥ ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the lover of the Vedas protect me in the evening; may the lord of flashing light protect me at midnight. May Sūrya, leader of the wheel of planets, protect me everywhere at all times. (27)

contentBlocks.original:
रणे राजकुले द्यूते विदादे शत्रुसङ्कटे ।
सङ्गामे च ज्वरे रोगे पातु मां सविता प्रभुः ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇē rājakulē dyūtē vidādē śatrusaṅkaṭē ।
saṅgāmē cha jvarē rōgē pātu māṃ savitā prabhuḥ ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In battle, in the royal court, in gambling, in legal disputes, in the danger of enemies, in combat, in fever, in disease — may Lord Savitṛ protect me. (28)

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ ॐ ॐ उत ॐउऔं ह स म यः सूरोऽवतान्मां भयाद्
ह्रां ह्रीं ह्रुं हहहा हसौः हसहसौः हंसोऽवतात् सर्वतः ।
सः सः सः सससा नृपाद्वनचराच्चौराद्रणात् सङ्कटात्
पायान्मां कुलनायकोऽपि सविता ॐ ह्रीं ह सौः सर्वदा ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ ōṃ ōṃ uta ōṃuauṃ ha sa ma yaḥ sūrō'vatānmāṃ bhayād
hrāṃ hrīṃ hruṃ hahahā hasauḥ hasahasauḥ haṃsō'vatāt sarvataḥ ।
saḥ saḥ saḥ sasasā nṛpādvanacharāchchaurādraṇāt saṅkaṭāt
pāyānmāṃ kulanāyakō'pi savitā ōṃ hrīṃ ha sauḥ sarvadā ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Om Om Om — may Sūra protect me from fear, he the embodiment of mantras. Hrāṃ hrīṃ hruṃ — may Haṃsa protect me on all sides. Saḥ saḥ saḥ — may Kulanāyaka Savitṛ protect me from kings, from forest-wanderers, from thieves, from battles, from all dangers. Om Hrīṃ Ha Sauḥ — always. (29)

contentBlocks.original:
द्रां द्रीं द्रूं दधनं तथा च तरणिर्भाम्भैर्भयाद् भास्करो
रां रीं रूं रुरुरूं रविर्ज्वरभयात् कुष्ठाच्च शूलामयात् ।
अं अं आं विविवीं महामयभयं मां पातु मार्तण्डको
मूलव्याप्ततनुः सदावतु परं हंसः सहस्रांशुमान् ॥ 30॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
drāṃ drīṃ drūṃ dadhanaṃ tathā cha taraṇirbhāmbhairbhayād bhāskarō
rāṃ rīṃ rūṃ rururūṃ ravirjvarabhayāt kuṣṭhāchcha śūlāmayāt ।
aṃ aṃ āṃ vivivīṃ mahāmayabhayaṃ māṃ pātu mārtaṇḍakō
mūlavyāptatanuḥ sadāvatu paraṃ haṃsaḥ sahasrāṃśumān ॥ 30॥
contentBlocks.translation: Drāṃ drīṃ drūṃ — may Taraṇi Bhāskara protect me from fear. Rāṃ rīṃ rūṃ — may Ravi protect me from fever, leprosy, and colic. Aṃ āṃ — may Mārtāṇḍa protect me from the terror of great disease. May Paraṃhaṃsa, the thousand-rayed one, whose body pervades the root syllable, always protect me. (30)

contentBlocks.original: अथ फलशृतिः
contentBlocks.transliteration: atha phalaśṛtiḥ
contentBlocks.translation: Now the Fruits of Recitation (Phalaśruti):

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीकवचं दिव्यं वज्रपञ्जरकाभिधम् ।
सर्वदेवरहस्यं च मातृकामन्त्रवेष्टितम् ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrīkavachaṃ divyaṃ vajrapañjarakābhidham ।
sarvadēvarahasyaṃ cha mātṛkāmantravēṣṭitam ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus this divine armor called the Diamond Cage, the secret of all the gods, encircled by the mother-syllables — (31)

contentBlocks.original:
महारोगभयघ्नं च पापघ्नं मन्मुखोदितम् ।
गुह्यं यशस्करं पुण्यं सर्वश्रेयस्करं शिवे ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahārōgabhayaghnaṃ cha pāpaghnaṃ manmukhōditam ।
guhyaṃ yaśaskaraṃ puṇyaṃ sarvaśrēyaskaraṃ śivē ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — which destroys the fear of great diseases, destroys sin, has issued from my own mouth, is secret, bestows glory, is meritorious, and confers all blessings, O Śivā. (32)

contentBlocks.original:
लिखित्वा रविवारे तु तिष्ये वा जन्मभे प्रिये ।
अष्टगन्धेन दिव्येन सुधाक्षीरेण पार्वति ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
likhitvā ravivārē tu tiṣyē vā janmabhē priyē ।
aṣṭagandhēna divyēna sudhākṣīrēṇa pārvati ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having written it on a Sunday, or on Puṣya nakṣatra or one's birth-star, O beloved, with the eight divine fragrances and nectar-mixed milk, O Pārvatī — (33)

contentBlocks.original:
अर्कक्षीरेण पुण्येन भूर्जत्वचि महेश्वरि ।
कनकीकाष्ठलेखन्या कवचं भास्करोदये ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arkakṣīrēṇa puṇyēna bhūrjatvachi mahēśvari ।
kanakīkāṣṭhalēkhanyā kavachaṃ bhāskarōdayē ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — with the holy milk of the arka plant, on birch-bark, O Mahēśvarī, with a golden pen, at the time of sunrise — (34)

contentBlocks.original:
श्वेतसूत्रेण रक्तेन श्यामेनावेष्टयेद् गुटीम् ।
सौवर्णेनाथ संवेष्ठ्य धारयेन्मूर्ध्नि वा भुजे ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvētasūtrēṇa raktēna śyāmēnāvēṣṭayēd guṭīm ।
sauvarṇēnātha saṃvēṣṭhya dhārayēnmūrdhni vā bhujē ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — wrapping the amulet in white, red, and dark thread, then encasing it in gold, one should wear it on the crown of the head or on the arm. (35)

contentBlocks.original:
रणे रिपूञ्जयेद् देवि वादे सदसि जेष्यति ।
राजमान्यो भवेन्नित्यं सर्वतेजोमयो भवेत् ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇē ripūñjayēd dēvi vādē sadasi jēṣyati ।
rājamānyō bhavēnnityaṃ sarvatējōmayō bhavēt ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One conquers enemies in battle, O Goddess; one wins in debates and assemblies; one becomes ever honoured by kings and becomes filled with all-encompassing radiance. (36)

contentBlocks.original:
कण्ठस्था पुत्रदा देवि कुक्षिस्था रोगनाशिनी ।
शिरःस्था गुटिका दिव्या राकलोकवशङ्करी ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṇṭhasthā putradā dēvi kukṣisthā rōganāśinī ।
śiraḥsthā guṭikā divyā rākalōkavaśaṅkarī ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Worn at the throat, the divine amulet grants sons; worn at the abdomen, it destroys disease; worn on the head, it brings all the people under one's sway. (37)

contentBlocks.original:
भुजस्था धनदा नित्यं तेजोबुद्धिविवर्धिनी ।
वन्ध्या वा काकवन्ध्या वा मृतवत्सा च याङ्गना ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhujasthā dhanadā nityaṃ tējōbuddhivivardhinī ।
vandhyā vā kākavandhyā vā mṛtavatsā cha yāṅganā ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Worn on the arm, it always bestows wealth and enhances radiance and intellect. For a woman who is barren, childless after one birth, or whose children die — (38)

contentBlocks.original:
कण्ठे सा धारयेन्नित्यं बहुपुत्रा प्रजायये ।
यस्य देहे भवेन्नित्यं गुटिकैषा महेश्वरि ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṇṭhē sā dhārayēnnityaṃ bahuputrā prajāyayē ।
yasya dēhē bhavēnnityaṃ guṭikaiṣā mahēśvari ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — she who always wears it at her throat will give birth to many sons. For whomever this amulet is always on the body, O Mahēśvarī — (39)

contentBlocks.original:
महास्त्राणीन्द्रमुक्तानि ब्रह्मास्त्रादीनि पार्वति ।
तद्देहं प्राप्य व्यर्थानि भविष्यन्ति न संशयः ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāstrāṇīndramuktāni brahmāstrādīni pārvati ।
taddēhaṃ prāpya vyarthāni bhaviṣyanti na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — all the great weapons, the Brahmāstra and others discharged by Indra, O Pārvatī, reaching that body shall become powerless — of this there is no doubt. (40)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं कवचं वज्रपञ्जरम् ।
तस्य सद्यो महादेवि सविता वरदो भवेत् ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikālaṃ yaḥ paṭhēnnityaṃ kavachaṃ vajrapañjaram ।
tasya sadyō mahādēvi savitā varadō bhavēt ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever daily recites this Diamond Cage armor thrice — at once, O Mahādēvī, Savitṛ becomes the bestower of boons to that person. (41)

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञात्वा कवचं देवि पूजयेद् यस्त्रयीतनुम् ।
तस्य पूजार्जितं पुण्यं जन्मकोटिषु निष्फलम् ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñātvā kavachaṃ dēvi pūjayēd yastrayītanum ।
tasya pūjārjitaṃ puṇyaṃ janmakōṭiṣu niṣphalam ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever worships Trayītanu (the Sun) without knowing this armor, O Goddess — the merit earned by that worship yields no fruit across millions of births. (42)

contentBlocks.original:
शतावर्तं पठेद्वर्म सप्तम्यां रविवासरे ।
महाकुष्ठार्दितो देवि मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śatāvartaṃ paṭhēdvarma saptamyāṃ ravivāsarē ।
mahākuṣṭhārditō dēvi muchyatē nātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites this armor a hundred times on the seventh tithi falling on a Sunday — one tormented by great leprosy is freed, O Goddess; of this there is no doubt. (43)

contentBlocks.original:
निरोगो यः पठेद्वर्म दरिद्रो वज्रपञ्जरम् ।
लक्ष्मीवाञ्जायते देवि सद्यः सूर्यप्रसादतः ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirōgō yaḥ paṭhēdvarma daridrō vajrapañjaram ।
lakṣmīvāñjāyatē dēvi sadyaḥ sūryaprasādataḥ ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: A healthy person who recites this armor, or a poor person who recites the Diamond Cage — by the grace of Sūrya, O Goddess, Lakṣmī swiftly comes to dwell with them. (44)

contentBlocks.original:
भक्त्या यः प्रपठेद् देवि कवचं प्रत्यहं प्रिये ।
इह लोके श्रियं भुक्त्वा देहान्ते मुक्तिमाप्नुयात् ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktyā yaḥ prapaṭhēd dēvi kavachaṃ pratyahaṃ priyē ।
iha lōkē śriyaṃ bhuktvā dēhāntē muktimāpnuyāt ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever devoutly recites this armor every day, O beloved Goddess — enjoying all prosperity in this world, they shall attain liberation at the end of life. (45)

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीरुद्रयामले तन्त्रे श्रीदेविरहस्ये
वज्रपञ्जराख्यसूर्यकवचनिरूपणं त्रयस्त्रिंशः पटलः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrīrudrayāmalē tantrē śrīdēvirahasyē
vajrapañjarākhyasūryakavachanirūpaṇaṃ trayastriṃśaḥ paṭalaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the thirty-third chapter, the exposition of the Sūrya Kavacham named the Diamond Cage (Vajra Pañjara), from the secrets of Śrī Devī in the Śrī Rudrayāmala Tantra.
